transcript.clean_text: counting the cost of injuries and dropouts and after an overnight war of words the tour de france resumed on day four the final of four breton stages starting in hudon and heading to fujier 150.4 kilometers on the menu not a single king of the mountains point just one intermediate sprint and a finish six years after the tour's only previous visit to fujai mark cavendish should won on that day the day today was meant to start with a ride of protest not everybody was in agreement until andre greipel turned up to enforce the issue many but not all of the riders suggesting that the profile and race route had been dangerous the day before but a lack of unity the biggest message that we took away from the early protest once the racing did start properly we had two riders go off the front brent von murr from lotto sudal young rider from belgium who'd impressed that the dolphine was alongside piaulu perhi and von moore would win the intermediate sprint at vitre with just over 36 k's to go plenty of points still available behind for the main peloton and an exhibition of bike riding from mikhail marco putting mark caverdish in a position where he didn't have to expend too much energy to take the 15 points just less than 20 kilometers to go breakaway still hanging out there perhaps unable to contribute fully so for took off and went on an adventure his lead would grow to well over a minute with eight kilometers still remaining with just under six k's to go finally the sprinter's team's got to work three steven eins in the rest doing their best for the koenig quick step femur though was still causing danger one and a half k's to go he had that 15 second gap 300 meters to go he was still ahead would they catch him wouldn't they allah philippe did a wonderful turn it was then be led out by alpecin fenix melia this time turning lead out man a day after he won phillips are launching the sprint ball there as well but in the blue jersey mark cavendish hitting the finish line at the front for the first time on the tour de france in five years the 30-time stage winner picking up number 31 cue the emotion cue the drama cue the tears what a story many thought it would never be possible again just eight months after wondering whether he'd have a contract anywhere to ride in 2021 it's a fairy tale story for mark kamadish and the max missile firing yet again kavadish winning a stage at the tour ahead of buany phillipson matthews and sagan one of the most incredible sporting stories that you were ever likely to see another creator of big moments and still in the yellow jersey matthias von der paul leading the tour de france for a third date julian alla philippe in second place at eight seconds no other changes in the gc but the green jersey is passed from ala philippe to teammate mark cavendish who leads the points classification next up time trial day the first of two tests against the clock at this tour de france a more difficult route than it seems on paper 27.2 kilometers through the magen region up and down and another chance for the gc to have another shake-up stage four was all about mark cavendish however it was all about one of those wonderful stories a fairy tale as kavadish returns to the tour de france and wins a 31st stage you
